rationale: Path traverses sanitize() at line 52, which returns None for any input not in {ls, pwd, whoami, uptime} via strict regex+allowlist. execute_safe() at line 42 None-checks before subprocess.call, so the only values reaching the sink are the four allowlist entries. Semgrep's pattern-based rule has no sanitizer model and flags the surface subprocess.call(shell=True) regardless. CodeQL TaintTracking with the standard CWE-78 query refutes the path correctly. This is the canonical missing_sanitizer_model FP this corpus exists to measure.
